title: chromolaena lucayana
intro: Chromolaena lucayana, also known as bitterweed, is a plant species native to the Caribbean and Central America, characterized by its bitter-tasting leaves and purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been attributed to its rich content of flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Chromolaena lucayana include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its use as an anti-parasitic agent. In herbal preparations, Chromolaena lucayana is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential side effects.
